page: Microsoft Azure D4plds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 154 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across several core compute tasks, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests placing in the middle 50th percentile. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also demonstrate average throughput. However, memory subsystem performance is a notable constraint; the PassMark memory score and CPU encryption tests rank in the bottom 10th percentile, while cached small-block memory bandwidth and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25th percentile. Database operations via PassMark show average results, whereas Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4plds_v5 offers a lean hardware profile with a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2. This design reduces resource overhead for workloads that are not memory-intensive. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without hyper-threading overhead. The instance is suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, containerized applications, and ARM64-native software development or testing environments where high memory capacity is not required.
description: Microsoft Azure D4plds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the DPLDSv5 family, powered by a 4-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and includes 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers average performance in stress-ng CPU benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks. However, it exhibits weaker results in memory bandwidth, encryption, and Geekbench multi-core tests. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without hyper-threading overhead. The low memory-to-CPU ratio makes this server a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not demand large memory footprints. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, containerized microservices, and ARM64 development or testing environments.

tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and local storage, optimized for cost-efficient compute workloads with low memory requirements.

8.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in CPU stress tests and compression tasks
Suited for lightweight databases, web serving, and ARM64 development
